dialogue.utterance: EMS to 3400 Adams Ave for overdose.
My name is Jordan. I understand your friend is unresponsive. Let me check on him.
Yes, he snorted something and passed out. He's breathing very slowly now.
Mr. Smith, can you hear me and squeeze my hand if you can?
Eyes are open but he's not looking at us. GCS is 6-E4, V1, M1 at first.
No verbal response. Minimal motor response when I tested his grip.
He barely moved when I touched his arm, but that wasn't purposeful at first.
Airway is patent. No signs of obstruction or sonorous respirations.
What's the depth and rate of his breathing? Any signs of work?
Shallow depth and occasionally agonal. Rate is 2-4 breaths a minute, roughly.
Sats at 20% on room air. Starting BVM at 15LPM O2.
Assist ventilations with BVM at about one breath every five to six seconds.
BVM is on, mask seal is tight, and I'm delivering breaths regularly now.
Good, let's keep monitoring his response. Does his breathing look more controlled?
He's holding 96% consistently with BVM supported breathing. Breathing does seem easier.
Skin is pale and cool. Can you check the pulses and heart rate?
Carotid and radial pulses strong and regular. Heart rate currently is 101 BPM.
Looks like he's improving in alertness. We'll stay on watch.
Can we test for pupillary reactivity? Both eyes open and round?
Pupils are 2mm, equal, and round. Reacting to light on both sides.
Let me check for rigidity, masses, swelling, or lymphadenopathy. Any findings?
No findings in neck or head. Patient appears fully mobile and unswollen now.
How does the skin feel? Temperature and turgor, please.
Blood pressure is 138 over 80 right now, and trending stable.
Blood glucose is 274-elevated but no signs of distress.
EKG shows sinus tachycardia with no life-threatening arrhythmias.
Tympanic temperature is 95.6F-slightly below normal.
Mr. Smith, are you feeling any chest pain, discomfort, or numbness?
No pain or discomfort at all. I'm fine now.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What led to this? What happened before you were found like this?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: He snorted heroin and passed out. I called 911 because he stopped breathing.
When was the last time you had something to eat or drink?
He had some beer last night, but no alcohol or food today.
Have we obtained any info from his belongings? Age, contact?
ID in his wallet confirms he's 21. No next of kin listed, as far as I can see.
You have a documented history of opioid use. Any other health concerns?
No other health problems I can recall.
Any known allergies or reactions to medications or foods prior?
No known allergies. Never had any reactions.
On a 0-10 scale, how severe is the pain or discomfort you're experiencing now?
I have no pain at all-zero discomfort.
When did the symptoms start, specifically?
Around 30 minutes ago. That's when he stopped breathing.
Earlier rate was around 7. Now, we see it's increased to 8-10 with BVM.
Heart rate dropped to 90, but it's regular and stable right now.
BP is up to 209 over 170-elevated and fluctuating now.
Next reading is 99. Seems more controlled over time.
Latest reading shows 11 breaths per minute. Breathing is less effortful now.
Activating protocol 3-15 for altered mental status and protocol 7-1 for opioid overdose.
Maintained sat levels at 94% and 97% post-intervention.
Rhythm is now sinus without any dangerous changes.
Blood glucose tested at 0, possibly due to sensor contamination.
Tried again with same result. May confirm with hospital for better testing.
Let's check breath sounds again with him more alert now.
Breath sounds clear and equal bilaterally. Some decreased intensity noted.
How is his breathing quality now he's more responsive?
Rate 12 and depth regular-with no signs of distress or gasping.
O2 holding steady at 94%. No need to adjust flow rate for now.
Are there any signs of pain or discomfort now that he's alert?
Still no pain. Breathing feels fine now.
Confirming destination-this patient will be taken to Chippenham Hospital at this time.
Secured on stretcher via five-point restraint. BVM and O2 still in place and functioning.
All equipment verified. He's stable and ready for movement according to protocol.
EMT, have we confirmed he is secured with seatbelts before transport begins?
Yes. He's buckled using both stretcher and ambulance restraints. All systems confirmed tight.
BP stable at 126/64. Readings steady during recheck.
Chippenham confirmed transport readiness. No contraindications noted.
hospital accepting patient. No reported complications with arrival.
All restraints and instruments rechecked. Patient and personnel secure for departure.
Completed pre-transport medications and assessments. All signs show readiness.
Final read of BP is 126/64. Transport can begin safely at this time.
Patient passed from stretcher to receiving bed with proper transition and reassurance.
All documentation and supplies turned over. Transport concluded without incident.
